zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth Le Despenser於1367年出生在英國英格蘭盧特藍德艾森丁時，她的父親Edward Despenser II31歲，母親Elizabeth de Burghersh25歲。她於1385年之前與Sir John Arundel結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1408年4月10日在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利享年41歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth Le Despenser于1367年出生在英国英格兰盧特藍德艾森丁时，她的父亲Edward Despenser II31岁，母亲Elizabeth de Burghersh25岁。她于1385年之前与Sir John Arundel结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1408年4月10日在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利享年41岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth Le Despenser was born in 1367, in Essendine, Rutland, England, United Kingdom, her father, Edward Despenser II, was 31 and her mother, Elizabeth de Burghersh, was 25. She married Sir John Arundel before 1385. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 10 April 1408, in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41, and was buried in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth Le Despenser est née en 1367, à Essendine, Rutland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edward Despenser II, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Burghersh, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Arundel avant 1385. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 10 avril 1408, à Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterrée à Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth Le Despenser 1367 in Essendine, Rutland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edward Despenser II 31 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de Burghersh 25. Sie hat vor 1385 Sir John Arundel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 10. April 1408 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth Le Despenser è nata il nel 1367 nel Essendine, Rutland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Edward Despenser II, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth de Burghersh, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Arundel prima del 1385. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 10 aprile 1408, nel Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolta a Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Le Despenserが1367年にイギリスイギリスルトランドエセンディンで生まれた時，父Edward Despenser IIは31歳，母Elizabeth de Burghershは25歳であった。1385年以前にSir John Arundelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1408年4月10日にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーで41歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーテウケスブリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Le Despenser(이)가 1367년 영국 잉글랜드 루틀랜드 에센딘에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward Despenser II(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Burghersh(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1385년 이전 Sir John Arundel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1408년 4월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth Le Despenser nasceu em 1367, em Essendine, Rutland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Edward Despenser II, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Burghersh, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Arundel antes 1385. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 10 de abril de 1408, em Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 41 anos, e foi sepultada em Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth Le Despenser родилась в 1367 г. в местности эссенден, рутланд, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Edward Despenser II было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Burghersh, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Arundel перед 1385 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 10 апреля 1408 г. в местности тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 41 год и была похоронена в местности тюкесбури эбби, тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elisabeth Le Despenser nació en 1367, en Essendine, Rutland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Edward Despenser II, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Burghersh, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Sir John Arundel antes de 1385. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 10 de abril de 1408, en Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultada en Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
